titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L252
GA L252
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Verse markers for 1.43-51 in M3D30b removed by David Parker.
verse end marker at 10.16 moved by David Parker.
Supplied text replaced by lac tags at 10.9 and 10.11 M1D2b by Rachel Kevern.
με πατρος moved from 6.39* to 6.38* and correction improved by Rachel Kevern.
Word break corrected at 3.16 by Rachel Kevern.
Beginning of 21.15-2 corrected to 21.14 by Rachel Kevern.
Ghost pages removed by Catherine Smith.
Word break at 7.20 corrected by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 D1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-Α number: L 252Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1rΒase text: TRnsΤranscribed by: S. Gibson; tags edited S. Gibson 07/09/12 Τranscription begun: 03/07/12Τranscription finished: 02/08/12 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - YesDiples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks: υιος is often written in full, with a superline.8 additional Johannine lections at end of MS.F360 is bound out of position, and goes after F37.S4 W2 D1 transcribed by acm.S5 W1 D5e transcribed by acm.S5 W1 D6h transcribed by acm.

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday
John 6,35-39 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D6)
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman
John 14,1-11a (Scrivener John 14,1-10; Gale John 14,1-11, Wake John 14,1-12)
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)
John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτoυργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
This lection is incorrectly ascribed to Mark.
John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)

ab.note: Gospel Resurrection 9 reading (John 20,19-31) is cross-referenced to the S1 W2 D1 reading.
Gospel Resurrection 11 reading (John 21,15-25) is cross-referenced to the S1 W7 D7 reading.

div.note: The beginning of this lection (15.1ff.) is lacunose.
In our lectionary index this lection is read on September 10th, but in this manuscript its number suggests that it is read on September 11th.
The heading instructs the user to read this pericope on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
Our lectionary index has John 19, 25-27; 21, 24-25 on this day.
Our lectionary index has Luke 6,17-23 or Matthew 11,27-30 on this day.
John 1, 35-51, up to v.42 in this MS.
Our lectionary index has Luke 14,33-42 on this day.
In our lectionary index this lection is read on December 12th, but in this manuscript its number suggests that it is read on December 10th.
Our lectionary index has Matthew 11,27 ff on this day.
Our lectionary index has Luke 4,22-30 on this day.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
This lection is read after the main reading.
One of a number of Pericopes for Various Occasions not mentioned in the Menologion.
One of a number of Pericopes for Various Occasions not mentioned in the Menologion.
One of a number of Pericopes for Various Occasions not mentioned in the Menologion.
One of a number of Pericopes for Various Occasions not mentioned in the Menologion.
One of a number of Pericopes for Various Occasions not mentioned in the Menologion.
One of a number of Pericopes for Various Occasions not mentioned in the Menologion.
One of a number of Pericopes for Various Occasions not mentioned in the Menologion.
One of a number of Pericopes for Various Occasions not mentioned in the Menologion.
